behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is driven by diarization/model output quality from the speech-to-text model (LLM-DEP applies to AI/ML output quality). An Integration test with mocked STT would likely always pass and hide mislabeled/missing speaker tags (MOCK-HIDE). Workflow was considered but rejected: this behavior text focuses on preserving labels for attribution, not validating a multi-step agent pipeline outcome end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Run a test with real multi-speaker meeting audio against the real STT model and verify speaker-labeled transcript segments are produced and retained in the payload consumed by summarization; assert attendee attribution in the produced summary matches those speaker labels for sampled utterances.

behaviors.description: The structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on extraction/completeness quality of real LLM output (LLM-DEP). Deterministic stubs cannot expose misses where the model omits an attendee’s task (MOCK-HIDE). Workflow was considered but rejected: the behavior is a single summary quality criterion, not inherently a chained multi-agent sequence requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real transcript fixture with known attendee-task assignments and call the real summarization model; parse structured output and assert each attendee with an assigned task in ground truth has at least one action item attributed to them.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the "decisions" section of the summary, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is about semantic extraction and section placement quality of real model output (LLM-DEP). Mocked responses would not reveal misclassification/format drift where decisions are omitted or misplaced (MOCK-HIDE). Workflow was considered but rejected: behavior text targets one model-output property, not multiple real agentic stages chained together.
behaviors.test_description: Feed real transcripts containing explicit decision statements into the real summarization model; assert each expected decision appears in the structured `decisions` field and does not appear only in freeform narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary spans orchestration across multiple sequential model calls (segment STT/summarization + merge), so MULTI-STEP applies. A lower-level test can validate splitting logic, but real coherence/regression failures often emerge only after chained agentic outputs are merged. System was considered but rejected because behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-step processing and merged coherent summary, which exceeds single-infra boundary checks.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute meeting recording, execute production segmentation and sequential processing with real STT+LLM calls per segment, then validate merged structured summary for cross-segment consistency (no dropped late-segment actions/decisions, stable attendee attribution, and valid final schema).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output — completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This behavior explicitly requires full end-to-end execution with real recording and chained AI stages (STT then summarization) plus delivery, so MULTI-STEP and REAL-INFRA both apply. Any lower level would miss cross-stage handoff and runtime orchestration failures that occur only in the complete pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end production-like test: upload a real meeting audio file, invoke real transcription and summarization services, and verify automatic delivery of valid structured output (action items, decisions, attributions) without human retries or manual patching.
